flashcards.definition: Amount increases by a fixed percentage each period: A = P(1+r)^t (investments, savings).
Amount decreases by a fixed percentage each period: A = P(1−r)^t (depreciation, purchasing power).
The decrease in value of an asset over time (cars, equipment, buildings) — typically modeled as exponential decay.
What a dollar can actually buy — decreases over time due to inflation (decay of money's value).
Time for an asset to lose half its value: t = ln(0.5)/ln(1−r).
